notes.text: Experts are invited to inspect John Hammond's new dinosaur theme park before opening.
A skeptical scientist forced into survival mode.
The visitors witness living dinosaurs and agree to tour the island despite ethical warnings.
Inciting event: human ambition crosses a natural boundary.
Malcolm vs. Gennaro: chaos theory skepticism vs. legal liability—foreshadows who survives chaos.
Park systems seem controlled at first, but tensions grow around security, weather, and corporate sabotage.
Visionary founder, torn between wonder and responsibility.
Goat on chain + absent T-Rex: classic suspense beat—absence of the monster is scarier than the money shot.
The first T-Rex attack destroys the illusion of control and shifts the story to pure survival.
Midpoint: nature reasserts dominance over engineered systems.
Power fails, staff are separated, and predators roam freely; every rescue attempt becomes riskier.
His betrayal triggers the collapse of the park's safety net.
The group commits to restoring power and escaping the island rather than waiting for rescue.
Break into Act III: survival demands collective responsibility.
Final clashes in the visitor center culminate with predators converging as the survivors race to escape.
Pragmatic, courageous, and crucial in restoring systems and protecting others.
Visitor center set piece: flag for production notes (practical vs. VFX) if you storyboard a remake.
The survivors leave the island, and Hammond accepts that the park cannot be controlled or opened safely.
Resolution: scientific power without humility leads to catastrophe.
